revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح اتاجو]][[cs:Svatyně Atago (Tokio)]][[en:Atago Shrine (Tokyo)]][[fr:Atago-jinja (Tokyo)]][[ja:愛宕神社 (東京都港区)]][[ru:Храм Атаго (Токио)]][[sv:Atago-jinja (Tokyo)]][[zh:愛宕神社 (東京都港區)]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Atago Shrine'''|愛宕神社|Atago Jinja|qq=}} | image = File:Atago jinja Tokyo main building.JPG | caption = Main building of the Atago shrine in Tokyo | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.664772|N|139.748421|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Atago|qid=Q4812642|1=Atago, Tokyo}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Atago Shrine'''|愛宕神社|Atago Jinja|qq=}} in {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} established in 1603 (the eighth year of the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} era) on the order of ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The current shrine buildings on the site date from 1958. The shrine is located on Atagoyama, a hill rising 26 meters above {{ill|Sea level|en|Sea level|qid=Q125465|lt=Sea level|1=Sea level}}. In old times, the shrine had a splendid view of Tokyo, now obscured by high rises. The very steep stairs leading to the shrine are also famous, as they represent success in life. According to legend, a young {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} dared to ride his horse up the stairs to deliver plum blossoms to the ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}''. It took his horse only one minute to get up, but 45 minutes to get down, and the horse was utterly exhausted afterwards. This scene is depicted in a painting in the main shrine hall. The shrine was erected to protect residents from fire, since its formerly excellent views were well suited to watch for fires, and therefore the main [[Kami|Shinto god]] worshipped in this shrine is the fire god ''[[Kagu-tsuchi|Homusubi no Mikoto]]''. Other gods worshipped are ''[[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome no Mikoto]]'' (a god of water), ''[[:en:Ōyamazumi|Ōyamazumi no Mikoto]]'' (a god of mountains), and ''{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}'' (a god of the military). ==See also== *[[Atago Gongen]] *[[:en:Atago Jinja (Kyoto)|Atago Jinja (Kyoto)]] ==External links== {{commons Category|Atago-jinja (Minato, Tokyo)|Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} *[http://www.atago-jinja.com/ Official website] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrine|qq=}} {{Atago Faith|qq=}}
[[image:Stairs to Atago jinja Tokyo.JPG|thumb|Stairs to the Atago shrine in Tokyo]] [[image:Brooklyn Museum - Atagoyama no nagame (Panoramic View of Atagoyama) - Kitao Shigemasa.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Atagoyama circa 1770, by Kitao Shigemasa]] [[image:Panorama of Edo bw.jpg|thumb|Panorama of Yedo (Tokyo) from Atagoyama, Felice Beato 1865]]
[[en:Atago Shrine (Tokyo)]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[arz:ضريح اتاجو]] {{Commons category|Atago-jinja (Minato, Tokyo)}} [[cs:Svatyně Atago (Tokio)]] [[fr:Atago-jinja (Tokyo)]] [[ja:愛宕神社 (東京都港区)]] [[ru:Храм Атаго (Токио)]] [[sv:Atago-jinja (Tokyo)]] [[zh:愛宕神社 (東京都港區)]] {{wikidata link|Q2868748}} [[Category:Atago shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]]
